Documentation loop starts from this build, not chat memory.

evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Fresh validation is preferred; policy may require it for high-risk semantic gates.

evidence: new session id or recorded context reset
Build contains accepted requirements, decisions, assumptions, open questions, and non-goals.
next_loop.handoff: Start documentation from this feedback build.

requirements.text: Product view must use progressive disclosure: default workspace shows user stories and UI surfaces first; users, source files, and deeper detail appear only after user choice or contextual need.
The right drawer may remain, but its default content must be valuable to users: meaning, alignment, evidence, warnings, and next safe action before backend paths or raw artifact data.
Board view should become a retro terminal-like Kanban board for active roadmap work, gates, blockers, and next actions.
Across CodeWiki UI, every surface should optimize for observability and alignment, reducing visible information until it helps answer intended state, actual state, gap, and next action.

evidence_mapping.criterion: Accepted user intent captured before documentation/code changes.
evidence_mapping.evidence: User accepted proposal and clarified right drawer/Kanban direction in chat on 2026-05-13.

summary: Accepted UI direction: make CodeWiki UI user-facing through progressive disclosure, valuable right source drawer, retro Kanban Board, and observability/alignment-first copy.

diff_table.current_state: Global right inspector exposes source paths, sections, and backend artifact detail by default.
diff_table.desired_state: Keep a right source drawer, but make it user-value-first: selected item meaning, alignment status, supporting evidence, warnings, and next action; raw paths and source text stay collapsed.
diff_table.rationale: The problem is not the drawer itself; the problem is backend-driven content with low user value.
diff_table.risk: May hide debugging details unless source expansion remains accessible.

diff_table.current_state: Product view shows users, stories, and UI surfaces together as equivalent categories.
diff_table.desired_state: Product landing shows User Stories and UI Surfaces first; users become filters/context inside focused story or UI detail.
diff_table.rationale: Progressive disclosure keeps Product focused on outcomes and visible surfaces instead of internal knowledge taxonomy.
diff_table.risk: User personas may become too hidden if filters are weak.

diff_table.current_state: Board renders task cards and metadata as a source-backed list/grid.
diff_table.desired_state: Board becomes a retro Kanban board with lanes such as Now, Ready, Blocked, and Done/Recent, emphasizing status, gates, blockers, and next safe action.
diff_table.rationale: Kanban maps work state to a familiar visual model while preserving the retro terminal feel.
diff_table.risk: Lane rules must stay deterministic and source-backed.

diff_table.current_state: System and Board details depend on the right bar even when the workspace should change around the selected thing.
diff_table.desired_state: Selections change the workspace to a focused view; the right drawer supports, not leads, the experience.
diff_table.rationale: Workspace should be the primary interaction surface; drawer should not force all views into a backend inspector pattern.
diff_table.risk: Requires breadcrumbs/back behavior to avoid disorientation.

diff_table.current_state: UI exposes many artifact classes and backend state fields because they exist.
diff_table.desired_state: Every visible element must support observability and alignment: intended state, actual state, gap/risk, or next action.
diff_table.rationale: This is the governing product rule for reducing noise without losing trust.
diff_table.risk: Requires pruning and copy changes across views.

accepted_decisions.summary: Retain a right source drawer, but redesign its content around user value and keep raw backend/source details collapsed by default.
Make Product default to User Stories and UI Surfaces; user/persona docs become contextual rather than primary default content.
Make Board a retro terminal Kanban surface for roadmap work.
Use workspace focus pages and breadcrumbs for progressive disclosure; the drawer supports selected context rather than driving every view.
Apply observability and alignment as the global UI pruning rule.

assumptions: Existing retro terminal visual direction remains desirable.
Raw canonical source access remains necessary for trust and debugging, but not as default content.
Initial implementation can be local browser UI only.
open_questions: Exact Kanban lane names and lane assignment rules can be refined during implementation.
Whether Product should show a small user filter row on landing or only inside opened story/UI detail remains a design detail.
non_goals: No hosted or multiplayer UI changes.
No direct graph or roadmap edits from the browser outside existing API capabilities.
